SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.equipment.compressor

Class AntiSurgeRecycleCalculator

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.equipment.compressor.AntiSurgeRecycleCalculator
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable
public class AntiSurgeRecycleCalculator extends Object implements Serializable
Steady-state anti-surge recycle calculator for a charted Compressor.

Given a compressor that already carries a performance map with an active surge curve and a natural (offtake) suction stream, this helper computes the cooled recycle flow required to hold the compressor inlet on the anti-surge control line in a converged steady-state flowsheet. It mirrors the field recycle topology (a Mixer that combines the natural suction with a cooled-gas recycle stream feeding the machine) and converges the recycle flow with a damped fixed-point iteration so that the machine inlet sits at the configured fractional distance to surge.

This is the steady-state companion to AntiSurgeController, which performs transient proportional-integral control of a recycle valve during dynamic runs. This class adds no new compressor physics; it only reads Compressor.getSurgeFlowRate() and Compressor.getDistanceToSurge() and re-runs the supplied compressor.

Typical usage: 

 Compressor comp = new Compressor("export", suction);
 comp.setUsePolytropicCalc(true);
 // ... attach chart + surge curve, set outlet pressure / solve speed ...
 comp.run();

 AntiSurgeRecycleCalculator calc = new AntiSurgeRecycleCalculator(comp, suction);
 calc.setSurgeControlMargin(0.05); // hold inlet 5% above surge flow
 calc.setRecycleCoolerTemperature(40.0, "C");
 AntiSurgeRecycleCalculator.Result r = calc.solve();
 if (r.isRecycleActive()) {
   double recycle = r.getRecycleStream().getFlowRate("MSm3/day");
   // comp.getPower(...) now reflects the recycled machine
 }

The recycle stream composition is cloned from the suction fluid. Because an ideal compressor does not change composition, this is an accurate representation of a cooled-discharge recycle while avoiding re-entrant coupling to the outlet stream during iteration.

Version:
1.0
Author:
NeqSim

    • logger

    • compressor

      private final Compressor compressor
      The compressor whose surge margin is being protected.

    • suctionStream

      private final StreamInterface suctionStream
      The natural (offtake) suction stream feeding the compressor before any recycle.

    • surgeControlMargin

      private double surgeControlMargin
      Surge-control margin as a fraction of the surge flow. The control line is surgeFlow * (1 + margin). Default 0.05 = 5%.

    • recycleCoolerTemperature

      private double recycleCoolerTemperature
      Recycle cooler outlet temperature value.

    • recycleCoolerTemperatureUnit

      private String recycleCoolerTemperatureUnit
      Recycle cooler outlet temperature unit (passed to the recycle stream).

    • maxIterations

      private int maxIterations
      Maximum number of fixed-point iterations.

    • tolerance

      private double tolerance
      Convergence tolerance as a fraction of the control flow. Default 0.005 = 0.5%.

    • damping

      private double damping
      Fixed-point relaxation (damping) factor in the range (0, 1].

    • lastResult

      private AntiSurgeRecycleCalculator.Result lastResult
      Result of the last solve() call.

  • Constructor Details

    • AntiSurgeRecycleCalculator

      public AntiSurgeRecycleCalculator(Compressor compressor, StreamInterface suctionStream)
      Constructs an anti-surge recycle calculator.
      Parameters:
      compressor - the charted compressor whose inlet is to be held on the surge control line
      suctionStream - the natural (offtake) suction stream feeding the compressor before recycle

  • Method Details

    • solve

      public AntiSurgeRecycleCalculator.Result solve()
      Solve for the steady-state cooled recycle flow that holds the compressor inlet on the anti-surge control line.

      The supplied compressor is run first to establish the natural operating point. If the natural inlet volumetric flow already meets or exceeds the control flow, no recycle is required and the compressor is left untouched. Otherwise a Mixer of the natural suction and a cooled recycle Stream is wired to the compressor inlet and the recycle flow is converged with a damped fixed-point iteration. On return, the compressor inlet reflects the converged recycled state so that Compressor.getPower(String) and related getters describe the recycled machine.

      Returns:
      the converged AntiSurgeRecycleCalculator.Result; never null

    • getSurgeControlMargin

      public double getSurgeControlMargin()
      Get the surge-control margin.
      Returns:
      the margin as a fraction of the surge flow

    • setSurgeControlMargin

      public void setSurgeControlMargin(double surgeControlMargin)
      Set the surge-control margin. The inlet is held at surgeFlow * (1 + margin).
      Parameters:
      surgeControlMargin - the margin as a fraction of the surge flow (e.g. 0.05 for 5%)

    • setRecycleCoolerTemperature

      public void setRecycleCoolerTemperature(double value, String unit)
      Set the recycle cooler outlet temperature.
      Parameters:
      value - the temperature value
      unit - the temperature unit accepted by the stream (e.g. "C", "K")

    • getRecycleCoolerTemperature

      public double getRecycleCoolerTemperature()
      Get the recycle cooler outlet temperature value.
      Returns:
      the temperature value in the configured unit

    • getRecycleCoolerTemperatureUnit

      public String getRecycleCoolerTemperatureUnit()
      Get the recycle cooler outlet temperature unit.
      Returns:
      the temperature unit

    • getMaxIterations

      public int getMaxIterations()
      Get the maximum number of fixed-point iterations.
      Returns:
      the maximum iteration count

      public void setMaxIterations(int maxIterations)
      Set the maximum number of fixed-point iterations.
      Parameters:
      maxIterations - the maximum iteration count

    • getTolerance

      public double getTolerance()
      Get the convergence tolerance.
      Returns:
      the tolerance as a fraction of the control flow

      tolerance - the tolerance as a fraction of the control flow (e.g. 0.005 for 0.5%)

      public double getDamping()
      Get the fixed-point relaxation (damping) factor.
      Returns:

    • setDamping

      public void setDamping(double damping)
      Set the fixed-point relaxation (damping) factor.
      Parameters:

    • getLastResult

      public AntiSurgeRecycleCalculator.Result getLastResult()
      Get the result of the last solve() call.
      Returns:
      the last result, or null if solve() has not been called
